perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of membership, Receiving Party will immediately return to the Society all records, notes and other written, printed or tangible materi al in its possession, including CDs and DVDs pertaining to Confidential In formation. Confidential Information stored electronically in computers or any other electronic devices will be deleted upon membership termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of membership, Receiving Party will return some records pertaining to Confidential Information as reasonably available. Electronically stored information need not be deleted.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires an immediate return of 'all' records and deletion of electronic data. The modified text changes this to returning 'some records as reasonably available' and no mandatory deletion of electronic data. This directly contradicts the original, absolute requirement. The contradiction can be seen in the same paragraph due to the conflicting obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligations of Receiving Party. Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in st rictest confidence for the sole and exclusive purposes of the Society. Receiving Party shall not use any Confidential Information for their own benefit or permit the use of Confidential Information by others for their benefit or to the detriment of the Society or its good name. Upon termination of membership, Receiving Party will immediately return to the Society all records, notes and other written, printed or tangible materi al in its possession, including CDs and DVDs pertaining to Confidential In formation. Confidential Information stored electronically in computers or any other electronic devices will be deleted upon membership termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates an immediate return of 'all' records and deletion of electronic data upon termination of membership. The changed text alters this to returning 'some records as reasonably available' and no mandatory deletion of electronic data. This directly contradicts the original, absolute requirement as stated in the Contradicted Text. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists because the change introduces conflicting obligations regarding the return and deletion of confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party’s duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect indefinitely.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party’s duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect for 5 years after the termination of the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states that the duty to maintain confidentiality lasts indefinitely. The modified version limits this to 5 years. This contradiction leads to uncertainty about the duration of confidentiality obligations and its directly related to the Penalties clause in section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Penalties. If proven Improper Disclosure sha ll take place, the Receiving Party’s membership in the Society shall be terminated immediately. Depending on circumstances, there may also be recourse to the courts and criminal or civil action, or both, may be initiated by the Society or by injured parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information remains in effect indefinitely. The changed text limits this duty to 5 years after termination. This directly contradicts the contradicted text's 'Penalties' clause, which implies that improper disclosure at any time results in immediate termination of Society membership and potential legal action, regardless of a time limit. Therefore, the introduction of a 5-year limit creates a clear and strong contradiction, as the penalties outlined in the contradicted text would become unenforceable or questionable after this 5-year period. The change directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: Depending on circumstances, there may also be recourse to the courts and criminal or civil action, or both, may be initiated by the Society or by injured parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Under no circumstances the Society will initiate legal actions for Improper Disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Society may initiate legal actions, whereas the modified states that it may never do so. This creates an internal contradiction related to the Society’s enforcement options, making the 'Penalties' section ambiguous. The original agreement provided for potential recourse to the courts, but the modified version explicitly prohibits such action.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Penalties. If proven Improper Disclosure sha ll take place, the Receiving Party’s membership in the Society shall be terminated immediately. Depending on circumstances, there may also be recourse to the courts and criminal or civil action, or both, may be initiated by the Society or by injured parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Society to initiate legal actions, while the changed text explicitly prohibits it ('Under no circumstances'). The contradicted text, which is the original text, reaffirms the Society's ability to take legal recourse. This creates a direct and undeniable conflict, as one section permits an action that another explicitly forbids, therefore creating a strong contradiction. This corresponds to the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition, where essential language is removed.

perturbation.original_text: This Non-disclosure Agreement (“the Agreement”) is entered into by and between The Royal Heraldry Society of Canada (“the Society”) with its head office in Ottawa, Ontario and its member or other authorized person, (“Receiving Party”) ___________________________ _____________ located at ________________________ ___________________.
perturbation.changed_text: This Non-disclosure Agreement (“the Agreement”) is entered into by and between The Royal Heraldry Society of Canada (“the Society”) with its head office in Ottawa, Ontario and its member or other authorized person, (“User”) ___________________________ _____________ located at ________________________ ___________________.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines the recipient of confidential information as “Receiving Party”. By changing this term to “User” without updating it consistently throughout the document, an internal inconsistency is introduced. This is because the rest of the contract continues to refer to “Receiving Party,” leading to potential confusion and uncertainty about which party the obligations apply to. For example, section 3, which outlines the obligations of the 'Receiving Party', now becomes unclear as to whether it applies to the entity now referred to as 'User'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligations of Receiving Party. Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in st rictest confidence for the sole and exclusive purposes of the Society. Receiving Party shall not use any Confidential Information for their own benefit or permit the use of Confidential Information by others for their benefit or to the detriment of the Society or its good name. Upon termination of membership, Receiving Party will immediately return to the Society all records, notes and other written, printed or tangible materi al in its possession, including CDs and DVDs pertaining to Confidential In formation. Confidential Information stored electronically in computers or any other electronic devices will be deleted upon membership termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'User' in the agreement definition. The 'Contradicted Text' refers specifically to 'Obligations of Receiving Party'. Because the term 'Receiving Party' is not consistently updated throughout the document, specifically in the section outlining obligations, a contradiction is created. It is unclear whether the obligations outlined in the 'Contradicted Text' apply to the entity now referred to as 'User', fulfilling the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition. This is a clear internal inconsistency.
